What we have before the Court today is the effort by the people of the state of Nebraska to balance the rights of the society to be free from crime and the fear of crime against an individual's right to liberty, and the people of the state of Nebraska suggest that the people's right to liberty -- that this means of doing that is reasonable, especially in light of the fact that we are only withholding the right to demand trial from those accused of heinous crimes, where the proof of their guilt is evident, and the presumption of their guilt is great.
